D6 Display L2 Understand Atom

Refresh Rate & Motion

a moving object is only redrawn so often — higher refresh rates leave tighter steps and smoother motion

A display shows motion as a series of still frames. At 60 Hz a fast object jumps a long way between frames, so the eye tracks a smear of widely-spaced positions — judder. Double the rate to 120 or 240 Hz and the same motion is sampled more finely, closing the gaps.

The ghost trail marks where the object was on recent frames at the chosen rate. At 60 Hz the ghosts are far apart and steppy; at 240 Hz they crowd into a smooth streak. Switch the rate and watch the spacing tighten.
